reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome — native Devanagari tokenizes to non-empty input_ids and inference returns real non-silent audio; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM). BEFORE building, the checkpoint's tokenizer_config.json was confirmed is_uroman:false with a 72-symbol native-Devanagari vocab (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor). Standalone probe: AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained('Xenova/mms-tts-hin') tokenized native Devanagari 'नमस्ते, यह हिंदी है।' to NON-EMPTY input_ids (len 37), then pipeline('text-to-speech','Xenova/mms-tts-hin',{dtype:'q8'}) returned a real Float32Array — 31232 samples @ 16000 Hz, peak 0.8501, mean |amp| 0.0856, 68.8% non-zero (non-silent).
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page. is_uroman:false means Devanagari is tokenised directly — no romanisation step, and no empty-input_ids crash.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = { audio: Float32Array (mono), sampling_rate: 16000 }. The probe returned exactly that shape; the page encodes the Float32 PCM to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio> and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Hindi speech (peak 0.8501).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language VITS demo: the Hindi checkpoint (own native Devanagari char vocab + Hindi-trained weights), materially different from the built English/German/Spanish/French/Arabic/Vietnamese mms-tts, and a different SCRIPT from all of them.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-hin, linked to its card), the original (facebook/mms-tts-hin), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx), size (~38 MB), license (CC-BY-NC 4.0) AND the is_uroman:false native-Devanagari fact. Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) noted in at-a-glance AND the footer byline.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (mount + model {modelId:'Xenova/mms-tts-hin', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:38}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-hin so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …'), not only the console.
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the Hindi textarea (lang=hi), Hindi sample chips (परिचय/अभिवादन/मौसम/समाचार/घोषणा/कविता), the बोलें button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains M2M100 (en→hi) + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built German/Vietnamese mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The standalone probe confirmed the exact non-silent audio the UI plays, from non-empty Devanagari input_ids.

All buttons have accessible names; the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Hindi speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live; Hindi content carries lang=hi for correct Devanagari rendering/announcement. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) AND desktop (1280×800), both light+dark. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread; keep any main-thread loop bounded.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis is in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); UI responsive during synthesis

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing; logical properties; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates and canvas labelling; Devanagari/Indic script rendering
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; give the waveform canvas an accessible name; mark language for correct script shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvas has aria-label; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names; Hindi textarea/chips carry lang=hi for correct Devanagari rendering.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; lang=hi on Hindi content

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the waveform canvas has aria-label describing it as generated Hindi speech; the numeric readout (samples/rate/duration) also conveys the same information as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text readout duplicate the visual

openQuestions: How much does the stochastic duration predictor vary Hindi prosody across sentence types (punchy vs flowing) — worth quantifying the spread distribution on the wild page.
Could a shared Indic sample-sentence set (numbers, dates, place names) be reused across the Hindi/Kannada demos to compare pronunciation quality per language?

followUpGoals.goal: Add more distinct-script Indic MMS-TTS demos (e.g. Tamil, Telugu, Bengali) once each config's is_uroman flag + native-script vocab are verified, to make the '1000+ languages, one recipe' claim tangible across scripts.
On the Hindi wild page, add a duration-spread histogram across sentence types to visualise where the stochastic duration predictor samples most freely.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-hindi (Xenova/mms-tts-hin, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the HINDI VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language, distinct-SCRIPT demo). CRITICAL pre-check: tokenizer_config is is_uroman:false with a 72-symbol NATIVE DEVANAGARI vocab (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: native Devanagari input tokenised to NON-EMPTY input_ids (len 37) and pipeline returned real non-silent Hindi audio (31232 samples @16000 Hz, peak 0.8501, 68.8% non-zero). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Ladder: basics (type Hindi, hear it), practical (Hindi narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (M2M100 en→hi translate, then MMS speaks the Hindi). MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
